---
title: "Standards and Metaevaluation"
description: "Study note on Standards and Metaevaluation in Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications."
book_id: "evaluation-theory-models-applications"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Major section"
source_pages: "353"
citation_key: "stufflebeam_coryn_2014_evaluation_theory_models_applications"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:philosophy-and-code-of-ethics-underlying-the-cipp-model:c0006"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2014</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Source pages 353</span><span>Major section</span></div>
This note summarises the MCP research-library section "Standards and Metaevaluation" from Daniel L. Stufflebeam and Chris L. S. Coryn's *Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications* [@stufflebeam_coryn_2014_evaluation_theory_models_applications]. It is a paraphrased study note based on source-linked retrieval records, not a substitute for the source text.

## Core Argument

The section connects CIPP to professional standards and metaevaluation.

## Study Summary

The section connects CIPP to professional standards and metaevaluation.

Evaluation quality is judged against utility, feasibility, propriety, accuracy, and accountability.

Metaevaluation is both formative, guiding correction during the work, and summative, judging final quality.

## Key Concepts

- Utility
- Feasibility
- Propriety
- Accuracy
- Evaluation accountability

## Source Location

- Section path: Part Three > Chapter 13 > Philosophy and Code of Ethics Underlying the CIPP Model
- Evidence records: `chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:philosophy-and-code-of-ethics-underlying-the-cipp-model:c0006`

## Practical Implications

- Use standards as checkpoints during the evaluation, not only after completion.
- Report how the evaluation met relevant quality standards.
- Seek independent metaevaluation when stakes justify it.

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Concepts: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Map: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)

## Study Prompts

1. What is the main evaluation problem that Standards and Metaevaluation helps diagnose?
2. Which assumptions would need evidence before using this guidance in a live evaluation?
3. How would this change the way an evaluator scopes questions, methods, or reporting?
4. Where could the guidance be misused if treated as a fixed template?

## References
